title: The Defeat of Cabracán, the Mountain-Shaker
summary: Commanded by Huracán (Heart of Heaven), the Hero Twins set out to destroy Cabracán, the boastful son of Vucub-Caquix who topples mountains. Posing as humble blowgun-hunters, they lure him eastward toward a great mountain and feed him a bird rubbed with white earth, which saps his strength. They then bind him and bury him in the earth, completing the destruction of Vucub-Caquix's proud family and closing Part I's cycle of punishing false greatness before the Twins' own birth is told.

nodes.label: The Earth-Rubbed Bird
nodes.description: A roasted bird the Twins rub with white chalk-earth and feed to Cabracán. Its odor whets his appetite, but the earth on it drains his strength so he can no longer move mountains; the magic object that works his ruin, sympathetically 'bringing him down to the earth' as it is itself covered with earth.

nodes.label: The East (Where the Sun Rises)
nodes.description: The eastern horizon, site of a great illusory mountain, toward which the Twins lure Cabracán at Huracán's command. The direction of dawn becomes the place of the prideful one's downfall.

nodes.label: The Defeat and Burial of Cabracán
nodes.description: The episode in which the Hero Twins lure, weaken with the earth-rubbed bird, bind, and bury Cabracán in the earth. It completes the destruction of Vucub-Caquix and his two sons and ends Part I's arc of humbling false greatness.

edges.description: Cabracán boasts that he demolishes and levels the mountains forever, an arrogant exaltation of his own power that marks him for destruction.
edges.evidence: "I demolish the mountains," he said; "I am leveling them to the ground forever."

edges.description: Huracán, Heart of Heaven, commands the Twins to bring Cabracán down because it is not right that he exalts his glory and grandeur on earth, setting the whole episode in motion.
edges.evidence: "Let the second son of Vucub-Caquix also be defeated. This is our will... Lure him to where the sun rises," said Huracán.

edges.description: Huracán together with Chipi-Caculhá and Raxa-Caculhá speaks the command to the Twins; they accept it as the will of Heart of Heaven, the peace.
edges.evidence: "Do you not exist, you who are the peace, you, Heart of Heaven?" said the boys as they listened to the command of Huracán.

edges.description: Hunahpú and Xbalanqué carry out the divine command, executing the scheme that lures, weakens, binds, and buries Cabracán.
edges.evidence: "In this way Cabracán was overcome by Hunahpú and Xbalanqué."

edges.description: The Twins pose as nameless poor blowgun-hunters and invent a great far-off mountain to lure Cabracán eastward and trap him.
edges.evidence: "We have no names... we are nothing more than shooters of blowguns and hunters with bird-traps on the mountains."

edges.description: The Twins use their blowguns to fell birds with mere puffs of air, awing Cabracán and supplying the bird that becomes his ruin.
edges.evidence: they felled the birds only with the puff of air when they shot them, which surprised Cabracán very much.

edges.description: The Twins roast a bird and rub it with white chalk-earth, fashioning the trap-food that will sap Cabracán's strength.
edges.evidence: they rubbed one of the birds with chalk, covering it with a white earth soil.

edges.description: Tempted by its savory smell, Cabracán eats the earth-rubbed bird; the earth on it weakens his legs and hands so he can no longer move the mountains.
edges.evidence: Cabracán's legs and hands were weakening... because of the earth with which the bird he had eaten was rubbed.

edges.description: The Twins march Cabracán east toward an illusory great mountain at the place where the sun rises, the bait that draws him to his end.
edges.evidence: "Over there it is, where the sun rises," said Hunahpú and Xbalanqué.

edges.description: The Twins bind Cabracán's hands, neck, and feet, throw him down, and bury him in the earth, overcoming the last of Vucub-Caquix's proud family.
edges.evidence: Then the boys tied him... Then they threw him to the ground and there they buried him.

edges.description: Cabracán's arrogant career of shaking mountains ends as he is reduced to a buried captive, his story resolving into the episode of his defeat.
Burying Cabracán fulfills the Twins' earlier resolve to destroy Vucub-Caquix together with both his sons.
edges.evidence: having first told of the destruction of Vucub-Caquix and that of Zipacná and of Cabracán.

edges.description: With the second son destroyed, the punishment of Vucub-Caquix's whole prideful lineage is now complete.
edges.evidence: "Let the second son of Vucub-Caquix also be defeated."

edges.description: Like Zipacná's defeat, Cabracán's follows the same pattern: the humble-seeming Twins use a deceptive lure and a fatal trick to overthrow a boastful mountain-being.
Cabracán's downfall mirrors his father's: in each case appetite and pride are turned against the proud one to bring him low.
The episode enacts the recurring theme that false self-exalted greatness must be humbled, here by burying the mountain-shaker in the very earth he boasted of leveling.
The Twins speak of the coming wisdom of created man 'when it dawns, when there is light'; clearing away the false, prideful beings prepares the world for the true dawn and the making of humankind.
edges.evidence: "Great shall be the wisdom of a created being... when it dawns, when there is light," said the boys.

edges.description: By ending the cycle of false greatness, the Twins' victory clears the stage for the births and the eventual creation of true humankind, the goal toward which the narrative now turns.
edges.evidence: "Now we shall tell of the birth of Hunahpú and Xbalanqué..."
